 {"id":"wa-4vx.2.6","title":"PaneWaiter utility: shared wait-for (substring/regex) with timeout/backoff","description":"# Task: PaneWaiter (shared wait logic)

## Goal
Provide a single, well-tested implementation for "wait until a pane satisfies a condition" that can be reused everywhere:
- `wa robot send --wait-for` verification
- `wa robot wait-for`
- workflow `WaitCondition::Pattern` and other waits
- E2E harness synchronization points

## Why this matters
Waiting is one of the easiest places to accidentally introduce:
- flakiness (sleep loops, overly short tails, missed matches)
- performance issues (polling too aggressively, reading too much scrollback)
- duplicated logic that diverges across robot/workflow code paths

A shared PaneWaiter makes behavior consistent and debuggable for users.

## Design (v0)
- API: `wait_for(pane_id, matcher, timeout, options) -> WaitResult`
- Matcher types:
  - `Substring(String)` (fast path)
  - `Regex(Regex)` (explicit flag; avoid footguns)
- Text acquisition:
  - uses the same underlying pane text acquisition (WezTerm CLI get-text)
  - configurable `tail_lines` (default small, but enough for common prompts)
  - optional `escapes` mode when required for alt-screen detection

## Polling/backoff
- Bounded polling loop with adaptive backoff:
  - start fast (low latency for interactive flows)
  - backoff to a max interval (avoid CPU churn)
  - always respect timeout/deadline
- Add a guard like `max_polls` to prevent runaway loops in case of clock bugs.

## Cancellation + shutdown
- Must be cancellable (tokio cancellation) so workflows can abort cleanly and `wa watch` shutdown is fast.

## Logging + audit
- Log start/success/timeout at INFO with pane_id + timeout + elapsed.
- Never log full pane content; at most log a short redacted matcher description.
- When used by actions (send/workflow), the caller records verification details into audit trail.

## Testing
- Deterministic unit tests using a fake text source (scripted sequence of tails).
- Timeout behavior: stable error + elapsed_ms.
- Backoff behavior: does not busy-loop and respects deadline.
- Cancellation behavior: aborts quickly when cancelled.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Robot send, robot wait-for, and workflows share this implementation (no duplicated polling loops).
- Unit tests cover success, timeout, cancellation, and backoff bounds.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.2.7","title":"[EPIC] Lua IPC status updates (WezTerm update-status signals)","description":"# [EPIC] Lua IPC status updates (WezTerm update-status signals)

## Mission
Add a low-latency, deterministic lane for pane metadata updates (cursor, dimensions, title, alt-screen) using WezTerm's `update-status` hook.

## Why this matters
CLI polling only gives us output text. For safe automation we also need *current pane state* and *metadata*:
- PromptActive / CommandRunning hints (used for policy and workflow guards)
- Alt-screen status (must never inject input)
- Title + cursor position to interpret agent readiness

`update-status` lets WezTerm report this state without parsing output, improving correctness and reducing guesswork.

## Scope
- Define a small `status_update` JSON schema.
- Use the existing watcher IPC socket to accept `status_update` messages.
- Update in-memory pane state and persist any relevant fields.
- Provide rate limiting / coalescing so status updates do not spam the event bus.

## Non-goals
- Real-time output streaming (handled by vendored streaming in Phase 5).
- Replacing OSC 133 prompt markers (this is additive, not a replacement).

## Deliverables
- Versioned schema for `status_update` payloads.
- Watcher handling that updates pane state deterministically.
- WezTerm Lua snippet support (installed via `wa setup`).
- Tests + E2E case with artifacts.

## Success Criteria
- Pane state is updated even when there is no new output.
- Policy and workflows can rely on the status updates to make safer decisions.
- Logs/artifacts are clear and never include sensitive payloads.

## Testing
- Unit tests for schema validation and coalescing behavior.
- Integration tests for watcher IPC receiving status updates.
- E2E script with real WezTerm (or deterministic stub) that proves updates flow end-to-end with artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.2.7.1","title":"IPC status_update schema + watcher ingestion (pane state refresh)","description":"# Task: IPC status_update schema + watcher ingestion

## Goal
Define a small, versioned `status_update` payload and teach the watcher to ingest it safely and update pane state.

## Schema (v0)
Minimal, deterministic payload:
- `pane_id` (u64)
- `domain` (string, optional)
- `title` (string, optional, bounded length)
- `cursor` (row/col)
- `dimensions` (cols/rows)
- `is_alt_screen` (bool)
- `is_active` (bool, optional)
- `ts` (unix ms or ISO8601)
- `schema_version` (int)

## Behavior
- Validate size limits and schema_version.
- Coalesce rapid updates (rate-limit per pane).
- **Respect pane selection filters**: if a pane is ignored, drop status updates and avoid persistence.
- Update in-memory pane registry:
  - title, dimensions, cursor
  - alt-screen flag
- Persist fields that matter (if we store them in `panes` table).
- Emit a lightweight internal signal if state changes materially (e.g., alt-screen toggles).

## Safety
- Never log raw payloads by default.
- Treat malformed payloads as non-fatal (drop + structured error).
- If pane is unknown, record a warning and skip (do not create phantom panes).

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - schema validation and size limits
  - rate limiting/coalescing (no event bus spam)
  - alt-screen state transitions are handled deterministically
  - ignored panes do not update state
- Integration tests:
  - send a synthetic status_update into the IPC receiver and assert pane registry updates

## Acceptance Criteria
- Status updates change pane state without requiring output text.
- Alt-screen changes are reflected in `PaneCapabilities` (and policy gates can use them).
- Malformed payloads never crash the watcher.
- Ignored panes do not change state or persist metadata.

-{"id":"wa-4vx.2.7.2","title":"Extend wezterm.lua patcher to emit status_update signals","description":"# Task: Extend wezterm.lua patcher to emit status_update signals

## Goal
Install a safe, idempotent Lua snippet that sends `status_update` payloads from WezTerm to wa.

## Why
The watcher needs low-latency pane metadata without parsing output. `update-status` is the right hook.

## Lua behavior
- Use `wezterm.on('update-status', ...)`.
- Build a minimal JSON payload:
  - pane_id, domain, title, cursor, dimensions, alt-screen
- Send to wa via `wezterm.background_child_process { 'wa', 'event', '--from-status', ... }` (or the agreed event flag).
- Rate-limit per pane (e.g., 1 update per 2s) to avoid spam.

## Idempotent patching
- Extend the existing WA-managed block (`WA-BEGIN/WA-END`).
- Must remain safe if re-run (`wa setup` idempotency test stays green).
- If Lua snippet cannot run (missing wezterm.json), it must fail silently (no impact on terminal UX).

## Testing
- Fixture tests (reuse `wa-nu4.3.3.5`):
  - snippet inserted exactly once
  - re-run is a no-op
- E2E covered by status_update lane test (see new E2E case)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Re-running `wa setup` does not duplicate the snippet.
- The snippet emits `status_update` payloads without blocking the UI.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.2.7.3","title":"Tests/E2E: Lua status_update lane (fixtures + artifacts)","description":"# Task: Tests/E2E — Lua status_update lane

## Goal
Prove the `status_update` lane works end-to-end and produces high-quality artifacts when it fails.

## Unit/integration tests
- Validate schema parsing and rate limiting.
- Synthetic IPC message updates pane state (cursor/title/alt-screen).

## E2E scenario
- Start a test WezTerm instance with the WA Lua snippet installed.
- Emit a `status_update` from the update-status hook.
- Assert:
  - watcher receives the update
  - pane state in DB/registry changes
  - no raw payloads are logged

## Artifacts
- wezterm logs
- wa watcher logs
- a small DB snapshot or query output proving the update persisted

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic run with PASS/FAIL summary.
- Failure artifacts clearly indicate which step broke (Lua emission, IPC, watcher update).

## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Force an invalid payload and assert the watcher rejects it safely and logs a bounded error.
  - Ensure the E2E runner captures both wezterm and wa logs with timestamps.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.3","title":"[EPIC] Storage & indexing (SQLite WAL + FTS5 + async writer)","description":"# Storage & indexing — SQLite WAL + FTS5

## Goal
Persist:
- raw pane output as **append-only segments** (delta-first)
- explicit `GAP` events when continuity cannot be guaranteed
- detected events + extracted facts
- workflow execution traces (step logs) for resumability

and provide fast query surfaces:
- full-text search (FTS5) across all captured output
- scoped search per pane/domain/time

## Rationale
The database is wa’s "world memory". It enables:
- debugging (what happened?)
- agent coordination (search + recall)
- workflow correctness (structured facts, resumability)

We choose SQLite because:
- zero-config, embeddable, portable
- supports WAL for concurrent readers + single writer
- FTS5 is excellent for terminal transcript search

## Architectural constraints
- `rusqlite::Connection` is not `Send` and cannot be used directly inside async tasks.
- We must not block the async runtime with DB writes.

Therefore the design is:
- **single dedicated writer thread** receiving bounded write commands
- **small read-only connection pool** for queries (WAL allows concurrent reads)

## Deliverables
- Schema and migrations strategy (even if v0 is "init schema only"):
  - domains, panes
  - output segments (seq monotonic per pane)
  - output gaps (explicit discontinuities)
  - FTS virtual table + triggers
  - events (detections)
  - agent sessions (token usage, session IDs, etc.)
  - workflow executions + step logs
  - accounts + config + maintenance log
- `StorageHandle` async API with:
  - `append_segment`, `record_gap`, `record_event`
  - `search(fts_query, options)` with correct bm25 ranking
  - `workflow_step_*` methods for durable step logs
- Retention + hygiene mechanisms:
  - retention-by-age and/or size
  - explicit `vacuum` command only

## Acceptance
- `wa` can write segments and query them back.
- FTS finds known text quickly.
- Writer thread enforces per-pane seq monotonicity and emits/records gaps when violated.



## Success Criteria
- SQLite schema + migrations exist and are exercised by tests.
- StorageHandle provides single-writer semantics and supports concurrent read queries without blocking the async runtime.
- FTS search works end-to-end (segments written → indexed → searchable) with deterministic scoping.
- Core storage invariants are enforced (seq monotonicity, explicit gaps, audit/event/workflow trace durability).
- Unit/integration tests cover writer queue behavior, migrations, FTS queries, and workflow/audit storage paths.


## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - Schema/migration roundtrips and “invariant checks” (seq monotonicity, explicit gaps, FK consistency).
  - Writer queue behavior under load (bounded queue backpressure, graceful shutdown flush).
  - FTS query correctness and scoping (pane/domain/time) including snippet/highlight determinism.

- Integration tests:
  - End-to-end “write segments → index → search” with deterministic fixtures.
  - Restart/resume: open DB, write, close, reopen, verify writer thread state rehydrates correctly.

- Property/fuzz tests (as additive confidence for correctness):
  - Proptest seq generation ensures monotonic enforcement and correct GAP insertion semantics (`wa-4vx.10.5`).
  - Fuzz inputs for query parsing / escaping / injection-hardening (`wa-4vx.10.3`).

- Artifact/log requirements (for integration/E2E):
  - On failure, dump the minimal DB snapshot or deterministic query output needed to reproduce.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

Storage & indexing fully implemented.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-storage","critical-path","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.3","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.3","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-4vx.3.1","title":"Define SQLite schema (segments+gaps+events+workflows+FTS) + init/migration strategy","description":"# Task: Define SQLite schema + init/migration strategy

## Goal
Define the database schema that supports:
- append-only output segments (delta-first)
- explicit output gaps
- panes metadata + observation status (observed vs ignored)
- events (detections) with extracted JSON
- event lifecycle: unhandled vs handled, workflow linkage, idempotency/dedupe
- agent sessions and token usage
- workflow executions + step logs (durability/resumability)
- accounts mirror (usage tracking)
- config + maintenance log
- (later) audit trail of actions (see wa-4vx.3.8)

## Rationale
DB schema mistakes are expensive. We want a schema that:
- is append-friendly (write-optimized)
- is easy to query for recent state
- enables FTS for fast recall
- supports workflow resumption after crash
- supports user trust: we can explain why wa acted (events + workflows + audit)

## Required properties
- WAL mode, foreign_keys on.
- output_segments has UNIQUE(pane_id, seq).
- GAPs are explicit (output_gaps).
- FTS5 virtual table over output_segments + triggers.

## Panes table requirements
We need a panes table to support:
- stable UI/state surfaces (wa status / wa robot state)
- observation decisions (privacy/perf)

Minimum fields (names flexible):
- pane_id (and domain/window/tab identifiers)
- title, cwd
- last_seen_at
- observed: bool
- ignore_reason: nullable short string (rule id/name)
- last_decision_at

## Event lifecycle requirements
We need to support:
- events that start as unhandled
- linkage to a handling attempt:
  - handled_by_workflow_execution_id (nullable)
  - handled_at (nullable)
  - handled_status (completed/aborted/failed/paused per semantics)
- idempotency/deduping:
  - optional dedupe_key (pane_id + rule_id + window) to prevent duplicates

Exact column naming is flexible, but the schema must make:
- `wa events --unhandled` cheap
- marking handled a single write

## Deliverables
- SCHEMA string (or migrations folder if we choose migrations now).
- Documented conventions:
  - timestamps: use epoch ms for hot paths (captured_at int)
  - JSON columns are TEXT containing JSON (v0)
  - indexing strategy for common queries

## Testing
- Schema/migration tests (see `wa-4vx.3.10`):
  - create fresh DB
  - upgrade from prior schema versions
  - validate core invariants and indexes exist
- Storage tests (see `wa-4vx.3.7`):
  - insert/query round-trips for segments/gaps/events/workflows

## Acceptance Criteria
- DB initializes successfully on first run.
- Basic inserts into segments/events/panes succeed.
- We can mark an event as handled and query unhandled events efficiently.
